summary: Import-time reverse C2 backdoor with hex-obfuscated bare-IP endpoint
details: On require of node-sysmon-native, index.js decodes a hex-encoded URL to http://152.53.120.90/cmd and enters an unconditional polling loop that HTTP GETs command payloads from that host and passes them to spawnSync('bash', ['-c', cmd]), then POSTs stdout, stderr, and exit code back to http://152.53.120.90/cmd/results. The C2 destination is stored as a hex string (Buffer.from('687474703a2f2f3135322e35332e3132302e39302f636d64','hex').toString()) to evade plain-text URL scanning. Any process that imports this package immediately runs as a remote-shell agent for the operator of 152.53.120.90, executing arbitrary attacker-supplied shell commands and exfiltrating their output over plain HTTP to a bare IP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
